rationale: Gemini has explicit search tooling docs while Claude and Codex are usable but not cited as first-class search surfaces here
Gemini documents headless mode explicitly and Claude or Codex remain strong but less specialized in cited sources
Gemini exposes checkpointing directly in cited docs while Claude and Codex are only generally resumable in this report context
Gemini explicitly documents sandbox and trusted folders while Claude security and OpenAI shell sandbox pages provide the supporting boundary evidence for their lower scores
Claude documents MCP as a core workflow and the other two remain strong but not the review default
Codex spans more user surfaces and Claude also has SDK level evidence while Gemini remains more CLI-centric in cited sources
Claude best matches rule-heavy review and independent scorer roles in SEE
Claude gets the edge because its MCP usage is central to governance workflows in this report
Claude sub-agents are explicitly documented and align with review separation
Internal SEE rules assign review and rule closure more naturally to Claude than to the execution-first tools
Codex has broader surfaces but that is not the deciding review criterion
Codex is the clearest execution-first fit in the cited official docs
Gemini scores high on sandbox docs but delivery remains dominated by code execution weight
Codex offers the broadest execution surfaces for delivery
Claude is strong on tooling but delivery is not tooling-led in the final route
Gemini headless strength matters but is secondary to code execution
Checkpointing is useful for delivery resiliency but not the dominant factor
Claude is closest to rule stewardship while Codex remains the delivery owner because execution weight dominates
